One-line pitch: A premium, cinematic cricket analytics platform that transforms complex IPL match statistics into engaging, AI-driven narratives and simplified insights for fans.
What does it do?:
CricBrain AI is a high-impact cricket dashboard that visualizes real-time match dynamics (e.g., KKR vs GT). Key features include:
• Momentum Timeline: A high-impact visualization of the ebb and flow of the match.
• Beginner Mode: AI-powered tooltips that explain technical jargon (CRR, RRR, Win Prob) using simple, relatable analogies.
• AI Sledge Mode: A toggleable personality for the AI agent (Analyst vs. Fanatic).
• Cinematic Player Cards: Dedicated impact profiles with dynamic AI ratings.
How is it agentic / how did you use AI?:
I integrated Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite to act as a multi-modal intelligent agent:
1. Dynamic Personas: The agent adapts its commentary style based on user preference—switching between a data-driven "Analyst" and a hyper-enthusiastic "Fanatic" in real-time.
2. Contextual Mentorship: It identifies technical terms in the UI and generates analogies tailored to the current match state to educate new fans.
3. Strategic Reasoning: The AI analyzes raw match JSON to generate structured strategic insights and "verdicts" on team performance.
4. Resilient Intelligence: Built with a hybrid layer that falls back to high-quality mock patterns if the AI service is unreachable, ensuring zero downtime.
Anything else judges should know?: Built specifically for the GDG Baroda APL Micro-Hackathon 2026. The project prioritizes "Aesthetics as a Feature," using premium animations and a dark-mode neon theme to simulate a high-end sports broadcast experience

One-line pitch: “LifeQuest AI — turn your real life into the greatest RPG ever played.”
What does it do?: LifeQuest AI is an AI-powered gamified self-improvement platform that transforms real-life habits, discipline, fitness, focus, and productivity into an immersive RPG-style experience with quests, boss battles, skill trees, evolving worlds, and cinematic progression systems.
How is it agentic / how did you use AI?: LifeQuest AI uses AI as an interactive growth mentor and progression engine. The AI analyzes user actions, habits, streaks, and behavioral patterns to generate motivational guidance, suggest personalized quests, simulate future growth paths, and create an immersive “main character” self-improvement experience. The system reacts dynamically to user progress through AI narration, adaptive responses, progression systems, and gamified feedback loops.
Stack & tools:
Next.js
React
Tailwind CSS
Framer Motion
Recharts
Lucide React
Context API + localStorage
Vercel (deployment)
Kiro AI (vibe coding & rapid prototyping)
Anything else judges should know?: LifeQuest AI was designed to emotionally immerse users into self-improvement rather than simply tracking habits. Instead of feeling like a productivity app, it feels like a playable cyberpunk RPG where users battle procrastination, evolve their avatar, unlock worlds, and level up their real life. The project was ideated, designed, and developed within the hackathon time limit with strong focus on cinematic UX, immersion, and psychological engagement.

One-line pitch: Turn your daily routine into a cosmic RPG adventure with AI-powered coaching, real-time focus battles, and a global leaderboard to level up your life.
What does it do?: It gamifies your daily routine by turning habits into cosmic quests, rewarding you with XP and level-ups for every task completed. The app fosters a social ecosystem through real-time collaborative focus rooms, habit battles with rivals, and a global leaderboard for competitive productivity
How is it agentic / how did you use AI?: Nova (powered by Gemini) acts as a persistent agent that consumes real-time user context—including habits, level, and XP—to provide personalized, adaptive coaching. The app proactively transforms your productivity patterns into active guidance, using AI to generate tailored insights and cosmic missions that evolve with your progress.
Anything else judges should know?: The app leverages real-time Firestore synchronization for collaborative focus rooms and live leaderboard updates, ensuring a zero-latency social experience. It features a premium, glassmorphism design with complex data-driven animations and a fully functional AI-integrated backend for personalized habit coaching.

One-line pitch: "LockIn: The high-stakes habit tracker where you duel your friends to see who stays disciplined the longest." ⚔️🔒🔥
What does it do?: What does it do? "LockIn is a gamified habit-tracking platform that turns personal discipline into a social sport. It features a unique 'Doodle' aesthetic where users create and track daily habits using custom Counters and Timers. To keep things resilient, we built a 'Shield' system that lets users protect their streaks on off-days. The standout feature is the Battleground, a 1v1 social arena where users can challenge friends to high-stakes habit duels to see who can stay 'Locked In' the longest." ⚔️🔒🔥
How is it agentic / how did you use AI?: "I used the Vibe Coding methodology, partnering with an Agentic AI Coding Assistant to handle the entire implementation. Instead of manually writing boilerplate, I directed the AI to build a custom design system, manage complex Supabase migrations, and implement the real-time 'Battle' logic. The AI acted as an autonomous technical partner—debugging database RLS policies and refactoring code on the fly while I focused on the product architecture and the 'doodle' vision."
Stack & tools:
Frontend: React 19, TanStack Router (Start), Tailwind CSS.
Backend: Supabase (PostgreSQL, Auth, Row-Level Security).
AI Tools: Antigravity, Claude, Stitch, Lovable
